content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: State and prove Sylow’s First Theorem.
Consider the symmetric group \(S_6\), and the corresponding alternating group \(A_6\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: How many elements of order 4 are there in \(S_6\)? Justify your answer.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: How many elements of order 4 are there in \(A_6\)? Justify your answer.

body.text: Let \(G\) be the alternating group \(A_{100}\), and assume \(G\) acts on a finite set \(\Omega\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that, if \(|\Omega|=90\), then \(G\) acts trivially on \(\Omega\), i.e. \(G\) has 90 orbits of size 1 on \(\Omega\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(|\Omega|=120\) and \(G\) does not act trivially on \(\Omega\), then \(G\) has, at most, 21 orbits on \(\Omega\).

body.text: Prove that every group of order 30 has a normal subgroup of order 15.
Let \(G\) be a group, let \(N\) be a normal subgroup of \(G\), and let \(H\) be a subgroup of \(G\). Assume that \(H\) is solvable. Prove that \(HN/N\) is a solvable group.
Let \(G\) be a group of order 39. Does \(G\) need to be abelian? Prove that your answer is correct.
